spec_match.rationale: The page now maps the visual flow to FedCM-shaped config, accounts, client metadata, assertion, validation, and navigator.credentials.get feature-probe behavior.

interactive.rationale: The walkthrough has step controls, a browser API probe, account/scope/auto-selection controls, and scenario selection for success and rejection branches.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still delivers the feature-index promise of a visual flow plus a live endpoint trace.

edge_cases.rationale: The trace can now exercise no-session, unknown-client, and account-mismatch failures and explains where the FedCM/RP flow stops.

references.rationale: The concept includes a direct chromestatus.com/feature/5141084139290624 link in its see-also section.

design_system.rationale: The new controls use public/styles.css variables, explicit text/background pairs, and button overrides keep foreground colors explicit.

openQuestions.title: Re-check labels as the origin-trial explainers settle
openQuestions.detail: The demo correctly labels unsupported browser APIs and falls back to the showcase IdP endpoints, but Agentic Federated Login is still described by multiple explainers. Revisit the scenario names when the ChromeStatus entry links to a stable spec section.

summary: The major ChromeStatus reference gap is resolved. The concept is now a hands-on FedCM flow debugger with configurable account, scope, auto-selection, browser probing, and explicit failure traces.
